description_long: This is an old, boxy cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a heavily weathered and scratched metal exterior, showing signs of rust and wear. The front features a large, convex glass screen with visible scratches, surrounded by a thick, metallic bezel. To the right of the screen is a circular speaker grill, and below the screen are four round control knobs, all of which are also rusted and aged. Several thick, red cables emerge from the right side of the front panel, adding to its industrial, neglected appearance. The top, sides, and back are plain, with a dull, grayish finish marred by scuffs and stains. This television looks like it has been abandoned or left outdoors for a long time, making it suitable for urban decay or post-apocalyptic settings.
description: A heavily weathered, vintage CRT television with a scratched glass screen, rusted metal casing, and exposed red cables on the front.
description_view_0: The left side of the television, showing a plain, scratched, and dirty metal surface with no visible features.
description_view_1: The front view displays the convex glass screen, rusted control knobs, circular speaker grill, and several exposed red cables.
description_view_2: The right side, similar to the left, is plain and heavily scratched, with no visible controls or features.
description_view_3: The back of the television, featuring a flat, gray, and scratched metal panel with no visible ports or details.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, metallic, gray, heavily scratched and rusted
attribute_control_button_layout: row of four round knobs below the screen on the front panel
